title: Today's AI Brief: How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex
summary: A brief look at Endava's use of Codex and Anthropic's new Opus 4.8 model.

body.text: I'm catching up on the latest AI news and there are some interesting developments. The field is moving so fast, it's hard to keep up. Companies are exploring new ways to use AI to improve their operations, and it's exciting to see the progress.

Endava, a software development company, is using Codex to build an agentic organization. This means they're leveraging AI to accelerate software delivery and reduce the time it takes to analyze requirements from weeks to just hours. That's a huge time savings, and I think it could significantly improve their development process and competitiveness.

Anthropic has released a new model, Opus 4.8. I'm not an expert, but it's clear that the company is making progress in the AI space. These models are getting better and more capable, which is exciting to see. I'm curious to learn more about it.
Another Update: More AI in the News
I'm also seeing more AI-related news, like updates on AI applications in various industries. It's clear that AI is having an impact across the board.

How Endava builds an agentic organization with Codex is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Learn how Endava uses Codex to build an agentic organization, accelerating software delivery and reducing requirements analysis from weeks to hours. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Anthropic releases new model, Opus 4.8 - Axios, from Axios, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Comprehensive up-to-date news coverage, aggregated from sources all over the world by Google News. Anthropic releases new model, Opus 4.8 Axios The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
